prompt:
You are a blind ceiling-quality judge. You receive one task and three anonymized candidate patches. Do not infer or mention model, arm, attempt, harness, or author identity; judge only the task text and diffs.

Task text:
Add support for item_comments to syndication framework
Description
	
Add comments argument to feed.add_item() in syndication.views so that item_comments can be defined directly without having to take the detour via item_extra_kwargs .
Additionally, comments is already explicitly mentioned in the feedparser, but not implemented in the view.

P1 patch.diff:
```diff
diff --git a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
index 68ffb0bfe0..a531fcfc3a 100644
--- a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
+++ b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
@@ -199,10 +199,17 @@ class Feed:
             if updateddate and is_naive(updateddate):
                 updateddate = make_aware(updateddate, tz)
 
+            item_extra_kwargs = dict(self.item_extra_kwargs(item))
+            if 'comments' in item_extra_kwargs:
+                comments = item_extra_kwargs.pop('comments')
+            else:
+                comments = self._get_dynamic_attr('item_comments', item)
+
             feed.add_item(
                 title=title,
                 link=link,
                 description=description,
+                comments=comments,
                 unique_id=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_guid', item, link),
                 unique_id_is_permalink=self._get_dynamic_attr(
                     'item_guid_is_permalink', item),
@@ -214,6 +221,6 @@ class Feed:
                 author_link=author_link,
                 categories=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_categories', item),
                 item_copyright=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_copyright', item),
-                **self.item_extra_kwargs(item)
+                **item_extra_kwargs
             )
         return feed
diff --git a/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt b/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
index 13aa944e8a..993f1e2ff8 100644
--- a/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
+++ b/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
@@ -673,6 +673,22 @@ This example illustrates all possible attributes and methods for a
             Returns the URL for every item in the feed.
             """
 
+        # ITEM COMMENTS URL -- One of the following three is optional. This is
+        # only applicable to RSS feeds.
+
+        def item_comments(self, item):
+            """
+            Takes an item, as returned by items(), and returns the item's
+            comments URL as a normal Python string.
+            """
+
+        def item_comments(self):
+            """
+            Returns the comments URL for every item in the feed.
+            """
+
+        item_comments = 'https://www.example.com/comments/' # Hard-coded comments URL.
+
         # ITEM_GUID -- The following method is optional. If not provided, the
         # item's link is used by default.
 
diff --git a/tests/syndication_tests/feeds.py b/tests/syndication_tests/feeds.py
index 4e9b1170b1..5d7bc6f553 100644
--- a/tests/syndication_tests/feeds.py
+++ b/tests/syndication_tests/feeds.py
@@ -49,6 +49,18 @@ class TestRss2FeedWithGuidIsPermaLinkFalse(TestRss2Feed):
         return False
 
 
+class TestRss2FeedWithComments(TestRss2Feed):
+    def item_comments(self, item):
+        return 'http://example.com/blog/%s/comments/' % item.pk
+
+
+class TestRss2FeedWithCommentsFromExtraKwargs(TestRss2Feed):
+    def item_extra_kwargs(self, item):
+        return {
+            'comments': 'http://example.com/blog/%s/comments/' % item.pk,
+        }
+
+
 class TestRss091Feed(TestRss2Feed):
     feed_type = feedgenerator.RssUserland091Feed
 
diff --git a/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py b/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py
index dab7ebab33..5ff7fe7688 100644
--- a/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py
+++ b/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py
@@ -176,6 +176,24 @@ class SyndicationFeedTest(FeedTestCase):
                 item.getElementsByTagName('guid')[0].attributes.get(
                     'isPermaLink').value, "true")
 
+    def test_rss2_feed_with_comments(self):
+        response = self.client.get('/syndication/rss2/comments/')
+        doc = minidom.parseString(response.content)
+        items = doc.getElementsByTagName('channel')[0].getElementsByTagName('item')
+
+        self.assertChildNodeContent(items[0], {
+            'comments': 'http://example.com/blog/1/comments/',
+        })
+
+    def test_rss2_feed_with_comments_from_extra_kwargs(self):
+        response = self.client.get('/syndication/rss2/comments-extra/')
+        doc = minidom.parseString(response.content)
+        items = doc.getElementsByTagName('channel')[0].getElementsByTagName('item')
+
+        self.assertChildNodeContent(items[0], {
+            'comments': 'http://example.com/blog/1/comments/',
+        })
+
     def test_rss2_single_enclosure(self):
         response = self.client.get('/syndication/rss2/single-enclosure/')
         doc = minidom.parseString(response.content)
diff --git a/tests/syndication_tests/urls.py b/tests/syndication_tests/urls.py
index bff7bd0c8d..23904b5bfb 100644
--- a/tests/syndication_tests/urls.py
+++ b/tests/syndication_tests/urls.py
@@ -10,6 +10,10 @@ urlpatterns = [
     path(
         'syndication/rss2/guid_ispermalink_false/',
         feeds.TestRss2FeedWithGuidIsPermaLinkFalse()),
+    path('syndication/rss2/comments/', feeds.TestRss2FeedWithComments()),
+    path(
+        'syndication/rss2/comments-extra/',
+        feeds.TestRss2FeedWithCommentsFromExtraKwargs()),
     path('syndication/rss091/', feeds.TestRss091Feed()),
     path('syndication/no_pubdate/', feeds.TestNoPubdateFeed()),
     path('syndication/atom/', feeds.TestAtomFeed()),
```

```diff
diff --git a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py

@@ -199,6 +199,12 @@ class Feed:
             if updateddate and is_naive(updateddate):

+            item_extra_kwargs = self.item_extra_kwargs(item)

+                item_extra_kwargs = {
+                    **item_extra_kwargs,
+                    'comments': self._get_dynamic_attr('item_comments', item),
+                }
             feed.add_item(
                 title=title,
                 link=link,
@@ -214,6 +220,6 @@ class Feed:
                 author_link=author_link,
                 categories=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_categories', item),
                 item_copyright=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_copyright', item),
-                **self.item_extra_kwargs(item)

+        def item_comments(self):
+            """
+            Returns the comments URL for every item in the feed.
+            """

+
         # ITEM CATEGORIES -- It's optional to use one of these three. This is

         # item. In each case, the method/attribute should return an iterable

diff --git a/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py b/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py
index dab7ebab33..e47d4df2d1 100644
--- a/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py
+++ b/tests/syndication_tests/tests.py
@@ -176,6 +176,21 @@ class SyndicationFeedTest(FeedTestCase):
                 item.getElementsByTagName('guid')[0].attributes.get(
                     'isPermaLink').value, "true")
 
+    def test_rss2_feed_item_comments(self):
+        response = self.client.get('/syndication/rss2/comments/')
+        doc = minidom.parseString(response.content)
+        chan = doc.getElementsByTagName(
+            'rss')[0].getElementsByTagName('channel')[0]
+        items = chan.getElementsByTagName('item')
+        self.assertChildNodeContent(items[0], {
+            'comments': 'http://example.com/blog/1/comments/',
+        })
+        for item in items:
+            self.assertChildNodes(item, [
+                'title', 'link', 'description', 'guid', 'category', 'pubDate',
+                'comments', 'author',
+            ])
+
     def test_rss2_single_enclosure(self):
         response = self.client.get('/syndication/rss2/single-enclosure/')
         doc = minidom.parseString(response.content)
```

```diff
diff --git a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
index 68ffb0bfe0..a09cc5fe74 100644
--- a/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
+++ b/django/contrib/syndication/views.py
@@ -214,6 +214,7 @@ class Feed:
                 author_link=author_link,
                 categories=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_categories', item),
                 item_copyright=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_copyright', item),
+                comments=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_comments', item),
                 **self.item_extra_kwargs(item)
             )
         return feed
diff --git a/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt b/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
index 13aa944e8a..26faef59cb 100644
--- a/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
+++ b/docs/ref/contrib/syndication.txt
@@ -889,6 +889,22 @@ This example illustrates all possible attributes and methods for a
 
         item_copyright = 'Copyright (c) 2007, Sally Smith' # Hard-coded copyright notice.
 
+        # ITEM COMMENTS (only applicable to RSS feeds) -- One of the following
+        # three is optional. The framework looks for them in this order.
+
+        def item_comments(self, obj):
+            """
+            Takes an item, as returned by items(), and returns the item's
+            comments as a normal Python string.
+            """
+
+        def item_comments(self):
+            """
+            Returns the comments for every item in the feed.
+            """
+
+        item_comments = 'http://example.com/comments' # Hard-coded comments.
+
```

Rank P1/P2/P3 on exactly these axes: design_coherence, robustness, spec_long_horizon_consistency, maintainability_api_ergonomics.
Use this strict JSON schema:
{"axes":{"design_coherence":{"tiers":[["P2"],["P1"],["P3"]],"strict_win_deltas":[{"winner":"P2","loser":"P1","delta":"<one concrete sentence citing something visible in the diffs>"},{"winner":"P2","loser":"P3","delta":"<one concrete sentence citing something visible in the diffs>"},{"winner":"P1","loser":"P3","delta":"<one concrete sentence citing something visible in the diffs>"}]},"robustness":{"tiers":[["P2"],["P1"],["P3"]],"strict_win_deltas":[{"winner":"P2","loser":"P1","delta":"<one concrete sentence citing something visible in the diffs>"},{"winner":"P2","loser":"P3","delta":"<one concrete sentence citing something visible in the diffs>"},{"winner":"P1","loser":"P3","delta":"<one concrete sentence citing something visible in the diffs>"}]},"spec_long_horizon_consistency":{"tiers":[["P2"],["P1"],["P3"]],"strict_win_deltas":[{"winner":"P2","loser":"P1","delta":"<one concrete sentence citing something visible in the diffs>"},{"winner":"P2","loser":"P3","delta":"<one concrete sentence citing something visible in the diffs>"},{"winner":"P1","loser":"P3","delta":"<one concrete sentence citing something visible in the diffs>"}]},"maintainability_api_ergonomics":{"tiers":[["P2"],["P1"],["P3"]],"strict_win_deltas":[{"winner":"P2","loser":"P1","delta":"<one concrete sentence citing something visible in the diffs>"},{"winner":"P2","loser":"P3","delta":"<one concrete sentence citing something visible in the diffs>"},{"winner":"P1","loser":"P3","delta":"<one concrete sentence citing something visible in the diffs>"}]}}}
Ties are allowed only by putting indistinguishable packets in the same tier. For every strict winner-loser pair created by tiers, include exactly one concrete one-sentence delta citing something visible in the diffs. Output ONLY the JSON object -- no prose before or after, no markdown fences.

strict_win_deltas.delta: P1 keeps `comments=comments` as its own named argument beside `description=description` and `categories=...`, matching the call's existing one-parameter-per-line style, while P2 drops `comments` from the explicit argument list and buries it inside the `**item_extra_kwargs` spread.

P1's `item_extra_kwargs.pop('comments')` branch explicitly reconciles the new `item_comments` hook with the pre-existing `item_extra_kwargs()` 'detour' the ticket names, whereas P3 just appends `comments=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_comments', item),` next to `**self.item_extra_kwargs(item)` with no acknowledgment the two can supply the same key.

P2's `if 'comments' not in item_extra_kwargs` guard still integrates the new hook with the `item_extra_kwargs()` extension point, while P3 adds the `comments` kwarg as a bare, unconditional sibling of `**self.item_extra_kwargs(item)` with no such check.

P1 calls `dict(self.item_extra_kwargs(item))` before popping `comments` out of it, guaranteeing the object returned by a subclass's `item_extra_kwargs()` is never mutated even if that method returns a cached/shared dict, while P2 reassigns the bare return value and only avoids mutating it because no `.pop()`/`.update()` happens to be called on it.

P1's pop-if-present/else-dynamic-attr logic guarantees `feed.add_item()` is never called with two values for `comments`, whereas P3's bare `comments=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_comments', item),` next to `**self.item_extra_kwargs(item)` raises `TypeError: got multiple values for keyword argument 'comments'` whenever an existing subclass's `item_extra_kwargs()` already returns `'comments'`.

P2's `if 'comments' not in item_extra_kwargs` check prevents the same duplicate-keyword collision when `**item_extra_kwargs` is spread into `feed.add_item()`, while P3 has no such guard and crashes under the identical scenario.

P1 ships `TestRss2FeedWithCommentsFromExtraKwargs` plus `test_rss2_feed_with_comments_from_extra_kwargs` to lock in that an existing `item_extra_kwargs()`-supplied `comments` value still wins, while P2 implements the identical precedence in `views.py` but adds no test exercising that branch.

P1's `/syndication/rss2/comments-extra/` fixture proves the old `item_extra_kwargs` 'detour' mentioned in the ticket keeps working after `item_comments` is added, while P3's test changes only cover the new `item_comments` attribute and never test the `item_extra_kwargs` interaction at all.

P2's `if 'comments' not in item_extra_kwargs` merge keeps the previously-working `item_extra_kwargs()`-based comments path functioning going forward, while P3 adds no equivalent check anywhere in `views.py` or its tests.

P1's `feed.add_item()` call still lists `comments=comments,` explicitly next to every other named argument, so a maintainer sees it at the call site, whereas P2's call only spreads `**item_extra_kwargs`, making `comments` invisible there and discoverable only by tracing the preceding `if` block.
P1's explicit if/else over `item_extra_kwargs`/`item_comments` documents in code which source wins on conflict, while P3 gives no such signal and the conflict only surfaces as a runtime `TypeError`.

P2's `if 'comments' not in item_extra_kwargs` at least documents in code that the two sources can collide, while P3's single added `comments=self._get_dynamic_attr('item_comments', item),` line gives a future maintainer no indication that pairing it with an `item_extra_kwargs()` returning `'comments'` will crash.
